greeting: Morning, Haniel. T28 crossed the line at 1:32 AM тАФ CT Pros is unblocked and today you ship them live.
hype: Everything's in position. Engine hardened, SDK at 0.2.0, key ready to provision. One focused push today and CT Pros is in production. Let's finish what you started.
notice: Phase A (CT Pros unblock) shipped overnight тАФ profiles API, auto-probe, wall height, SDK 0.2.0 all landed. Go-live is fully unblocked.

tasks.title: CT Pros go-live (provision key on VPS ┬╖ engine redeploy ┬╖ ct-pros deploy)
tasks.notes: Everything is LOCAL-ONLY so far. Checklist: run service/provision.py тАШCT ProsтАЩ on VPS -> their .env TAKEOFF_API_KEY; redeploy engine (new routes); build+deploy ct-pros bundle; verify [takeoff-react] v0.2.0 console stamp in prod.

prep.what: Deploy CT Pros to production: provision their API key on the VPS, redeploy the updated engine, and push the ct-pros bundle live.
prep.understand: Phase A (t28) shipped at 1:32 AM тАФ the engine now has the profiles API, auto-probe, wall-height PATCH, merge-dissolve, and SDK 0.2.0. Three separate deploys are needed: (1) provision the CT Pros key in the engine DB, (2) pull and rebuild the engine on the VPS, (3) build and push the ct-pros Laravel/Inertia app. The provision CLI must never receive the key inline тАФ use `--key-stdin`.
approach: SSH into the AZTEAM VPS, cd /opt/takeoff-engine, run `python -m service.provision 'CT Pros' --key-stdin` and save the plaintext key (printed once only).
Drop the plaintext key into the ct-pros `.env` as `TAKEOFF_API_KEY`.
On the VPS: `git pull && docker compose -f docker-compose.lean.yml up --build -d`. Hit the health endpoint through TLS to confirm the new routes are live.
Build and deploy the ct-pros bundle (run deploy scripts, migrate, seed). In a browser logged in as Admin, open the Takeoff module and verify the `[takeoff-react] v0.2.0` console stamp appears.

tasks.title: Label lane v2: M3 vision for outlined-text firms + callout-vs-room filtering
tasks.notes: Caught live on Harborview demo: rooms show тАШunnamedтАЩ because PDFTron outlines ALL text (55 live words, zero room labels). v2 = (a) productize M3 tiles lane as opt-in тАШread labels (AI)тАЩ action + (b) callout-vs-room filtering.

prep.what: Build the M3-tiles label lane as an opt-in service action and add a callout filter so stair arrows, note paragraphs, and FEC callouts stop matching as room names.
prep.understand: The free live-text lane finds nothing on PDFTron-outlined firms because all glyph paths are stroked, not readable text. The M3 tiles spike (run_labels_m3tiles.py) already works тАФ it slices the sheet into raster tiles and calls MiniMax vision (~4.3 min/sheet, costs credits). The MINIMAX_AI key needs a secrets decision to reach VPS (same pattern as API_KEYS_RAW: chmod-600 .env). Holabird shows the noise problem: тАШUPтАЩ, тАШFEC 1AтАЩ, multi-line note paragraphs all match as room names.
approach: Wire the M3 tiles lane as `POST /sheets/{id}/label-ai` behind a MINIMAX_AI env guard; dispatch a ┬з7a metered-credits event on each call.

Add a callout-vs-room classifier: filter hits matching known noise patterns (single uppercase letters, short alphanumeric codes тЙд5 chars, multi-line spans, directional keywords). Start rule-based; tune on Holabird noise examples.
Add MINIMAX_AI to the VPS .env secrets (same chmod-600, comma-separated pattern). Document in README-SERVICE.md secrets section.
Smoke-test on Harborview (should go from all-unnamed to labeled) and re-run Holabird (should keep 92/109 named, drop callout noise).
resources: MiniMax AI vision API docs (multimodal tiles endpoint)
Harborview demo PDF + Holabird baseline (92/109 named)

tasks.title: Assemblies-per-room тЖТ paint/drywall/flooring quantities + wall heights
tasks.notes: PRD Phase 1. assemblies-lite folded into B1 (engine/assemblies.py scaffolded). Full version = room type тЖТ assemblies + formulas + heights (one classification тЖТ up to 8 quantities, CSI libraries). The last pillar before estimator-grade Excel.

prep.what: Expand engine/assemblies.py from the lite scaffold to a full classificationтЖТquantity matrix so every room row in the Excel gains paint SF, wall SF, flooring SF, and CSI trade quantities.
prep.understand: The scaffold exists (engine/assemblies.py) but has only placeholder logic from B1. The full version takes a roomтАЩs classification + measured area + perimeter + wall height and produces up to 8 derived quantities per CSI trade division (e.g., Division 09 Finishes: wall paint SF = perimeter ├Ч height, ceiling SF = area, floor SF = area). Wall height is a job-level input тАФ it doesnтАЩt come from the 2D plan. Quantities flow through `build_sheet_takeoff` into the 5-tab Excel workbook and downstream into ct-pros `TakeoffEstimateSeeder.php`.
approach: Define the classificationтЖТassembly matrix in engine/assemblies.py: map 5-6 core room types (bedroom, bathroom, kitchen, living/common, corridor, storage) to quantity formulas. Each formula takes `(area_sf, perimeter_lf, height_ft)` and returns a named-quantity dict.
Implement `assemble_room(classification, area_sf, perimeter_lf, height_ft) тЖТ dict` and update `build_sheet_takeoff` to call it per room row, appending quantity columns to the sheet tab.
Add a `wall_height_ft` parameter to the job/sheet model (default 9 ft) that the worker passes through to `assemble_room`.
Run the gauntlet тАФ verify 85/85 still bit-identical (assembly columns are additive, no engine-touching). Spot-check Holabird: bedroom should show ~floor_area SF flooring + perimeter├Ч9 SF paint.

tasks.title: Fill real CT Pros company contact info
tasks.notes: config/company.php + .env still hold HighTech placeholders. Set real CT Pros name/address/phone/email BEFORE any client-facing PDF or email goes out.

prep.what: Replace all HighTech Tile placeholder data in config/company.php with real CT Pros contact details before any PDF or email reaches a client.
prep.understand: The app was cloned from HighTech Tile Inc. тАФ every client-facing PDF (estimate, proposal, invoice, change order) and email still shows HighTech branding. `config/company.php` is the canonical source read by all PDF renderers. There may also be a logo asset thatтАЩs still HighTechтАЩs. This must be corrected before the first real client interaction.
approach: Open config/company.php and list every key: name, address, city, state, zip, phone, email, website, logo_path.
Get real CT Pros info from Rafa: legal business name, address, phone, email, website.
Update config/company.php + any logo asset at the path referenced in logo_path.
Generate a test estimate PDF and confirm it shows CT Pros name, address, and logo тАФ not HighTech.
resources: app/Pdf/ or resources/views/pdf/ (PDF templates referencing company data)
CT Pros real contact info from Rafa

tasks.title: Connect CT ProsтАЩs own QuickBooks (fresh OAuth)
tasks.notes: Inherited tokens were HighTechтАЩs and were never carried over. Run fresh OAuth against CT ProsтАЩs own QBO company so all syncs hit the right books.

prep.what: Complete a fresh QuickBooks OAuth flow in the deployed CT Pros app to link their own QBO company file and replace the dead HighTech tokens.
prep.understand: The OAuth infrastructure is complete and tested. The tokens in the DB belong to HighTechтАЩs QBO account and cannot sync CT ProsтАЩs books. The OAuth flow: /auth/quickbooks/redirect тЖТ Intuit consent screen тЖТ /auth/quickbooks/callback тЖТ stores token. Confirm QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID, QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET, and QUICKBOOKS_ENVIRONMENT=production are correct in .env before starting.
approach: Check .env: confirm QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_ID, QUICKBOOKS_CLIENT_SECRET, and QUICKBOOKS_ENVIRONMENT=production are set.
Log into the CT Pros app as Admin, go to Settings тЖТ QuickBooks, click Connect.
Complete the Intuit OAuth flow тАФ select CT ProsтАЩs actual QBO company file when prompted.
Verify the Settings page shows тАШConnectedтАЩ and a QuickBooksToken row is saved. Optionally trigger a manual sync to confirm no API errors.
resources: Intuit Developer portal (app credentials: client ID + secret)
CT Pros QBO company login credentials

tasks.title: Re-curate material catalog for a GC
tasks.notes: The 148-SKU HighTech TILE catalog still seeds тАФ wrong domain. Replace with a GC-appropriate catalog before takeoff pricing or estimates are trusted.

prep.what: Replace the 148-SKU HighTech tile catalog with a GC catalog covering CT ProsтАЩs actual trades: painting, flooring, trim, and subbed materials.
prep.understand: CuratedMaterialSeeder.php seeds the catalog Rafa sees when building estimates. It currently lists tile products (HighTechтАЩs domain). CT Pros self-performs painting and flooring тАФ so the catalog needs: interior/exterior paints by coverage, LVP/hardwood/ceramic/carpet flooring by type, primer, caulk, trim (per LF). Takeoff pricing (seed-from-takeoff seam) will reference this catalog to price estimate lines тАФ wrong SKUs = wrong prices from day one.
approach: Open CuratedMaterialSeeder.php and audit the 148 SKUs: mark tile-specific SKUs for deletion, identify paint/flooring items to keep or expand.

Build a replacement set of 10тАУ15 core SKUs: interior latex paint (per gallon, ~400 SF coverage), primer, ceiling paint, LVP flooring (per SF), hardwood, ceramic tile, carpet, baseboard trim (per LF), caulk/supplies. Use CT Pros supplier pricing from Rafa.
Update CuratedMaterialSeeder.php and WalkThroughDefaultMaterialSeeder.php (which references product type surfaces). Run `php artisan db:seed --class=CuratedMaterialSeeder` on staging.
Open the app тЖТ Settings тЖТ Catalog and verify new items appear with correct names and prices.
resources: CT Pros supplier price list (from Rafa)

tasks.title: Deploy CT Pros to its own host/domain
tasks.notes: Deploy infra proven (deploy/*.sh, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md) but still pointed at HighTechтАЩs server. Re-target to a fresh droplet or Hostinger with CT ProsтАЩs domain + fresh DB.

prep.what: Provision a fresh server for CT Pros, run the proven deploy scripts, and get the app live at CT ProsтАЩs domain with a real DB and correct .env.
prep.understand: The deploy pipeline is complete (deploy/*.sh, docker-compose.yml, docs/deployment.md) тАФ it was proven for the HighTech clone. This is a fresh-target deploy: new server, new domain, fresh DB (no HighTech data). YouтАЩll build .env from the template using values already gathered today: TAKEOFF_API_KEY (provisioned in t30 this morning), company info (t24), QB keys (t25). Allow 30тАУ60 min including DNS propagation.
approach: Provision a fresh DigitalOcean droplet (or Hostinger). Point CT ProsтАЩs domain DNS A record at the new server IP.
Clone the ct-pros repo on the server. Copy .env.example тЖТ .env and fill in all values: APP_URL, DB credentials, QB keys, TAKEOFF_API_KEY, mail config.
Run `bash deploy/deploy.sh` (per docs/deployment.md). Apply migrations (`php artisan migrate`). Seed the catalog (`php artisan db:seed`).
Verify: load CT Pros domain in browser, log in as Admin, generate a test estimate PDF (confirms company branding), open the Takeoff module (confirms `[takeoff-react] v0.2.0` console stamp).
resources: CT Pros domain registrar (DNS A record)
